posts.name: Gary Schulte
posts.cooked: While watching NHK World this evening they had a business program on alcohol with a short segment on Hokkaido wineries specializing in pinot noir … Pinot Noir wine: Hokkaido is now one of the most famous regions in Japan, but mimicking Burgundy isn't the idea | NHK WORLD-JAPAN News . Turns that Etienne de Montille has thrown his interest into the area and opened up a new winery in the area …https://www.demontille-hokkaido.com/en . They mentioned that the wine have a savory umami flavor. Has anyone tried a pinot noir from northern Japan?

link_counts.title: Pinot Noir wine: Hokkaido is now one of the most famous regions in Japan, but mimicking Burgundy isn't the idea | NHK WORLD-JAPAN News

posts.cooked: I’ve heard Domaine Takahiko Pinot Noir Nana-Tsu-Mori is quite good, but haven’t been able to try it yet (I was just in Hokkaido last Decemeber and May).

posts.name: Mads Wildgaard
posts.cooked:
A few weeks ago I had the Coco Farm Pinot Noir;
A project out of Hokkaido. They work with local organizations that employ disabled people to pick grapes and other things. こことあるシリーズ ココ・ファーム・ワイナリー公式オンラインショップ
They work with a guy called Bruce Gutlove it seems.
I’ve yet to have the Takahiko.

What bottle of wine did you open today? (Part 2) WINE TALK
[IMG_7865] Saalwächter - Weiss Burgunder 2022 Bold but well-composed are the first thoughts. A lot of beeswax and ripe yellow fruits. I’m debating wether to call this waxy or banana. Super supple and bright at first sip with a lot of minerality, which is followed by a somewhat restrained acidity considering how young the wine is. The oak notes are rather dominant and there’s a salty element here too. Blinded I’d have said good Burgundy or Jura. For 23€ this is good value! [IMG_7867] Coco …

Leon & Son Wine and Spirits

2020 Domaine Takahiko, Clos da Descion Pinot Noir, Hokkaido, Japan

From Murakami vineyard.  Planted n steep south-facing slopes with minimal top soil.  10hl/ha.

Price: USD 149.99

of course unscrupulous sellers may ask for as much as they wish! it is often said that w-s listings are the prices at which wine does not sell

posts.name: Otto Forsberg
posts.cooked:
Gary_Schulte:

They mentioned that the wine have a savory umami flavor.

We had a tasting of Japanese reds a little while ago. Almost every single wine had a strong, savory, meaty umami flavor. No matter which variety the wine was made from, that meaty umami note was there. I wonder if it is from the winemaking practices, terroir, or what?

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: I’ve had a few Pinot Noirs from Hokkaido. Not sure I’d quite go as far as umami, which is of course a taste as opposed to a flavor, but all have had what I would describe as a sort of maritime/brine-y/kelp-y flavor, which is probably amplified by stem retention. They made me think of a hypothetical red Muscadet (and yes, I know Pinot Noir is actually grown in the Pays Nantais; and no, it doesn’t taste like this, so it’s a pure thought experiment).

posts.name: Otto Forsberg
posts.cooked:
William_Kelley:

Not sure I’d quite go as far as umami, which is of course a taste as opposed to a flavor, but all have had what I would describe as a sort of maritime/brine-y/kelp-y flavor, which is probably amplified by stem retention.

Good call; it is not a flavor, but definitely a taste. Flavor would be something you could also smell, because it is based on volatile compounds, detected retronasally. There is nothing atypical in the aromas of these wines - but no matter how the wine smells like, you get that very noticeably savory umami taste.

I could get behind that kelp taste, because kelp is typically very umami-rich in taste. But the wines we tasted didn’t have any maritime / briney flavors.

posts.name: Мајкл Ристев
posts.cooked: i wonder how a whole cluster pinot from the finger lakes such as heart and hands would stack up blind compared to some of these hokkaido pinots. i feel like fruit weight and density (or lack therefore of) could be similar.

posts.name: Charlie Gierling
posts.cooked: Any single vineyards we should add to weinlagen-info?

posts.name: Otto Forsberg
posts.cooked:
Charlie_Gierling:

Any single vineyards we should add to weinlagen-info?

Checked out your maps of Japan. The wine regions were pretty huge compared to where the vines grow. Most of the covered region was on Japanese Alps, ie. impassable mountains.

posts.name: Robert Dentice
posts.cooked: That is an interesting comparison. I have had both and it did not occur to me. I actually think the Finger lakes should focus more on Pinot than riesling. The Pinots can be really unique fully ripe 12 % abv wines at fair prices. A bit like the Mosel Pinot I love. They do have to tone down the oak.

posts.name: William JR Tipton
posts.cooked: Coincidentally I just had a taste of the 2022 Takahiko Nana-Tsu-Mori last night. My first thoughts were that it had a lot of acid, was savory, and there was a maritime salinity to it. While I would agree that I didn’t experience any totally novel aromas, the overall expression was new to me and was pleasantly puzzling. Definitely had it as cooler climate wine, and with some whole cluster.

posts.name: Charlie Gierling
posts.cooked: Right. I think they are just the administrative Regions, not actually those where the the wine grows. If interested in correcting and adding information, just drop me a PN. Help is always welcome.
